body: Closes #628. I've also spread the pattern of using `do` syntax in hopes to prevent further spread of these bugs.
title: Fix post processing memory leaks

body:
# PULL REQUEST

## Purpose and Content
Generalises the hyperdiffusion tendency with projection operations. 

(Provide references to any link issues. Use closes #issuenum to automatically close an open issue)
- #408 

## PR Checklist
- [x] This PR has a corresponding issue OR is linked to an SDI.
- [x] I have followed CliMA's codebase [contribution](https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/) and [style](https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/) guidelines OR N/A.

- [x] Unit tests are included OR N/A.
- [x] Code used in an integration test OR N/A.
- [x] All tests ran successfully on my local machine OR N/A.
- [x] All classes, modules, and function contain docstrings OR N/A.
- [x] Documentation has been added/updated OR N/A.
title: Update hypdiff tendencies (follows on from 540)

body:
This seems to fix #627, I'm not exactly sure why, but I'm not super familiar with how `atexit` works in a distributed context (assuming that's the reason).

It'd be nice if we could add some sort of assertion, but maybe not necessary as global scope is decluttered.
title: Fix distributed logger

body:
Everything printed before MPI logging is configured will print for all processors, which is noisy. This PR moves the output printing to after the MPI logging is configured.

cc @jiahe23
title: Mv print output dir to after logging config

body: This PR declutters global scope a bit more by adding a function to get the prognostic state. Another step towards #341.
title: Declutter global scope

body: covar dissipation is used only for diagnostics, as the equation is solved implicitly
title: remove covar dissipation

body: This PR moves some variable definitions into a new function, `ode_config`, to help further declutter the global scope.
title: Declutter global scope
